SUMMARY:
@AvinashAbbigeri **Looks good to merge 👍.**
**Thanks for contributing 🚀 🚀.**
You may join our _Discord server_ - **https://discord.gg/DeT4jXPfkG** to get updates about the project.
v5:
SUMMARY:
This PR adds a legal-use disclaimer to the README and creates a new disclaimer page on the website, linked from the footer. The new page follows the existing pattern used by the privacy-policy and terms pages (fetching raw GitHub content at build time), but introduces concerns around the external URL dependency and scope creep in package-lock.json.

POINTS:
- [Website/app/disclaimer/page.tsx:4] The fetch URL points to `SaptarshiSarkar12/Drifty/master/disclaimer.txt` (the author's fork). The PR description acknowledges this will be updated post-merge, but consider updating it to a commit-pinned URL (e.g., `SaptarshiSarkar12/Drifty/{commit-hash}/disclaimer.txt`) before merge — the privacy-policy page already pins to a commit hash (`c86ce857...`), which prevents breakage if the file is deleted or modified on `master`. The terms page does not pin and is similarly fragile.
- [Website/app/disclaimer/page.tsx:1-15] No error handling on the `fetch()` call (no `response.ok` check, no try/catch, no fallback). This matches the existing privacy-policy and terms pages, so it's not a regression — but all three legal pages will crash the build if the GitHub raw URL becomes unreachable (404, rate-limit, network). Consider adding at least a `if (!response.ok) throw new Error(...)` guard across all three in a follow-up.
- [disclaimer.txt] Missing trailing newline (`\ No newline at end of file` in the diff). Add one to comply with POSIX conventions and avoid git merge artifacts.
- [README.md:89-96 vs disclaimer.txt:1-8] The disclaimer text is duplicated between the README section and `disclaimer.txt`. These could drift out of sync. Consider dropping the README copy in favor of a link to `/disclaimer`, or explicitly noting they are mirrors.
- [Website/package-lock.json] The diff includes an unrelated bump of `@eslint/plugin-kit` (0.3.1 → 0.3.4) and a nested `@eslint/core` update. This is not part of the disclaimer feature. Recommend reverting this file so the PR stays scoped.
- [Website/components/Footer.tsx:16] The change from `space-x-6` to `space-x-4` is a reasonable visual adjustment for the extra disclaimer link. No objection — noted for awareness.
v6:
SUMMARY:
This PR adds a legal-use disclaimer to the README and creates a new /disclaimer page on the website. The page fetches disclaimer content from a GitHub raw URL. While the change is straightforward, there are several reliability and consistency issues — most notably the absence of error handling for the external fetch, which is already a known gap in the existing privacy-policy and terms pages.

POINTS:
- [Website/app/disclaimer/page.tsx:3-4] **Missing error handling on external fetch.** The `fetch()` call has no try-catch or `response.ok` check. If GitHub's raw CDN is unavailable or the URL 404s, the page crashes at build/SSG time with no graceful fallback. The `download/page.tsx` page demonstrates the project's own robust pattern: try-catch, `response.ok` check, and an error-state UI with a retry button. Apply the same pattern here (and consider applying it retroactively to privacy-policy and terms pages).
- [Website/app/disclaimer/page.tsx:3] **PR description contradicts the code.** The PR notes "Disclaimer text is currently loaded from my fork. Once this PR is merged, we can update the link to point to the main repo." However, the URL already points to `SaptarshiSarkar12/Drifty` (the main repo), not a fork. Clarify which is correct — either the description is outdated, or the URL needs to be changed.
- [disclaimer.txt:8] **Missing trailing newline.** The file ends without a newline character (`\ No newline at end of file` per the diff). This can cause issues with text processing tools and is a POSIX convention violation. Add a final newline.
- [README.md:89-95 vs disclaimer.txt:1-8] **Duplicated disclaimer content.** The same disclaimer text appears in both README.md and disclaimer.txt. If the legal wording needs updating in the future, both files must be changed. Consider pointing the README to the website's disclaimer page instead of duplicating the text, or consolidate into a single source of truth.
- [Website/components/Footer.tsx:16] **Incidental spacing change.** The `space-x-6` to `space-x-4` modification to the footer nav is unrelated to the disclaimer feature. If intentional, call it out in the PR; if unintentional, revert to keep this PR focused.
